meta.model: Gravitas Valuation Intelligence — Weighted Scoring & Scenario Analysis
meta.methodology: Weighted multi-factor scoring with 3-scenario NPV/multiples analysis. Weights derived from Alpine AI Valuation Parameter Framework (Critical=4, High=3, Medium=2, Low=1). All monetary values in CHF unless noted. Projections clearly separated from confirmed data.
meta.dataDisclaimer: All source parameters taken from the Alpine AI Valuation Parameter Framework. Comparable transaction multiples are framework-defined ranges, not independently sourced market data. Awaiting DEE-17 (Research Agent) for external comparable validation.
assumptions: Tax rate: 15% effective (Swiss cantonal rate assumption — requires verification)
Depreciation: 7% of CapEx annually (20-year useful life, straight-line, rounded)
Working capital: negligible relative to asset base
Revenue ramp: Year 1-2 at stated utilisation, steady state from Year 3
No terminal value modelled — 20-year PPA horizon used as project life
Discount rates: WACC 8% base (6-12% range per framework)
All scenarios use Phase 1 (25MW) except Bull which includes full 55MW entitlement

weightedScoringModel.methodology: Each of 30 parameters rated 1-5, multiplied by weight factor (Critical=4, High=3, Medium=2, Low=1). Maximum possible score = 5 × sum(weights). Scores represent relative positioning vs market peers.

parameters.name: Contracted Power Rate
parameters.rationale: 58-69% below market rate on a 20-year locked contract — exceptional cost advantage

parameters.name: PPA Duration & Structure
parameters.benchmark: Typical 5-10 year PPAs with annual escalation
parameters.rationale: Duration and flat structure eliminate long-term energy cost risk entirely

parameters.name: Behind-the-Meter Structure
parameters.benchmark: Most facilities grid-connected; BTM carries 15-25% NPV premium
parameters.rationale: BTM avoids grid fees, transmission losses, and regulatory tariff risk

parameters.name: PUE Target
parameters.rationale: 28% lower overhead energy consumption; separate CHF 39M NPV line item

parameters.name: Power Capacity — Phase 1
parameters.rationale: Solid initial scale; not yet hyperscale but sufficient for enterprise AI workloads

parameters.name: Permitting Status
parameters.benchmark: Most projects 12-36 months to full permits
parameters.rationale: Eliminates permitting risk and time-to-revenue delay entirely

parameters.name: Full Entitlement
parameters.rationale: Expansion path fully entitled — no future permitting uncertainty

parameters.name: Exclusive Development Rights
parameters.rationale: Competitive moat — no rival can develop the same site

parameters.name: Location — Jurisdiction
parameters.benchmark: Tier-1 sovereign jurisdiction for data sovereignty
parameters.rationale: Swiss legal framework, political stability, data protection laws

parameters.name: Climate Suitability
parameters.rationale: Cold climate reduces cooling energy, directly supports PUE 1.08

parameters.name: Energy Counterparty Quality
parameters.dataPoint: EKW, Alpiq, Axpo, BKW, Canton GR
parameters.benchmark: All sovereign-grade Swiss utilities / cantonal entities
parameters.rationale: Near-zero counterparty default risk — sovereign and quasi-sovereign entities

parameters.name: Customer Pipeline
parameters.rationale: Pre-revenue stage; pipeline not publicly disclosed. Significant uncertainty.

parameters.name: Revenue Diversification
parameters.rationale: Concentration risk from single site and single service model

parameters.name: Contract Structure Target
parameters.rationale: Target model is contracted enterprise — adds revenue visibility if achieved

parameters.name: Sovereign Tenant Potential
parameters.rationale: Swiss sovereign AI narrative attracts government-adjacent customers

parameters.name: CapEx Efficiency
parameters.rationale: At market for Swiss jurisdiction — premium reflects local construction costs

parameters.name: Target EBITDA Margin
parameters.rationale: Power cost advantage directly drives 10-20pp margin premium over peers

parameters.name: Revenue per MW
parameters.rationale: Above-average revenue density for enterprise colocation

parameters.name: WACC / Cost of Capital
parameters.rationale: 8% is market-appropriate for Swiss infrastructure with pre-revenue risk

parameters.name: Utilisation Ramp
parameters.benchmark: Aggressive but achievable for well-located facilities
parameters.rationale: Reasonable ramp if customer pipeline materialises

parameters.name: Cooling Technology
parameters.dataPoint: Alpine free-cooling + advanced cooling supporting PUE 1.08
parameters.rationale: Best-in-class cooling efficiency from natural + engineered approach

parameters.name: AI Infrastructure Readiness
parameters.rationale: Greenfield purpose-built design avoids retrofit compromises

parameters.name: Network Connectivity
parameters.rationale: Graubünden is not a primary connectivity hub — latency to major exchanges unclear

parameters.name: Redundancy & Uptime Target
parameters.dataPoint: Not specified — assumed Tier III equivalent
parameters.rationale: No stated tier classification; assumed Tier III based on enterprise positioning

parameters.name: Sustainability Credentials
parameters.rationale: Strong green credentials from Swiss hydro sourcing

parameters.name: Development Stage
parameters.rationale: Permits secured is significant de-risking but still pre-revenue

parameters.name: Founder / Management Team
parameters.benchmark: Track record in similar projects is key
parameters.rationale: No independent assessment of team — neutral rating pending due diligence

parameters.name: Regulatory Relationships
parameters.rationale: Cantonal engagement and utility partnerships signal strong regulatory position

parameters.name: Capital Raising Progress
parameters.rationale: No disclosed funding status — significant execution uncertainty

parameters.name: Construction Readiness
parameters.benchmark: Most greenfield projects still in design phase
parameters.rationale: Shovel-ready status eliminates design and planning delays

overallScore.interpretation: Strong overall positioning driven by exceptional power/energy infrastructure and site/permitting scores. Below-average scores in counterparty (pre-revenue risk) and execution/team (undisclosed funding, unassessed management) represent the primary discount factors.

categoryRanking.insight: Perfect score — fully permitted, sovereign jurisdiction, exclusive rights
Near-perfect — exceptional power economics drive the value thesis
Strong cooling/sustainability, gaps in connectivity detail and tier classification
Margin projections excellent but CapEx at market and utilisation is projection
Energy counterparties excellent but customer pipeline undisclosed
Shovel-ready but funding and team track record unverified

bull.label: Bull Case
bull.description: Full entitlement developed, high market rate, maximum multiples

probabilityAssessment.rationale: Requires full 55MW buildout, sustained high market rates, and maximum valuation multiples. Historically aggressive.

base.label: Base Case
base.description: Phase 1 only, current market benchmark, mid-range multiples

probabilityAssessment.rationale: Reflects framework parameters at face value with Phase 1 scope. Most assumptions align with stated targets.

bear.label: Bear Case
bear.description: Phase 1 only, compressed market rates, lower multiples

probabilityAssessment.rationale: Market rate compression to CHF 0.18/kWh narrows spread. Higher CapEx and WACC reflect execution risk. Lower utilisation from slower ramp.

valuationMetricsEngine.methodology: Metrics calculated from scenario assumptions. Revenue = capacity × revenuePerMW × utilisation. EBITDA = revenue × margin. EV = EBITDA × multiple. NPV uses discounted cash flows over project life at scenario WACC. All values in CHF millions unless noted.

npvAnalysis.note: Negative net NPV at full 55MW CapEx shows capital intensity. Value is captured through multiple expansion (EV/EBITDA) and strategic premiums, not pure DCF on CapEx vs cash flow.
Infrastructure projects typically show negative CapEx-to-DCF NPV early. Value realisation depends on multiple-based exits and platform premium.
Bear case with higher CapEx per MW and lower utilisation shows significant CapEx recovery risk.

probabilityWeightedEV_CHF_M.interpretation: Probability-weighted enterprise value of CHF 363M reflects base-case dominance with bear-case drag.

premiumDiscountAttribution.methodology: Each premium/discount quantified as percentage adjustment to base multiples or as standalone NPV line item. Premiums are cumulative but subject to overlap dampening (total capped at reasonable bounds). All figures are estimates based on framework parameters — not independently validated market data.

premiums.name: Swiss Sovereign Jurisdiction Premium
premiums.rationale: Swiss jurisdiction provides data sovereignty, political stability, and regulatory clarity. EU-adjacent but non-EU regulatory independence valued by sovereign and enterprise clients.
premiums.source: Framework parameter: sovereign-grade counterparties + Graubünden location

premiums.name: Power Cost Advantage NPV
premiums.rationale: Direct spread between contracted rate (CHF 0.10/kWh) and market benchmark. This is the single largest value driver.

premiums.name: BTM Structure Premium
premiums.rationale: Framework states 15-25% NPV premium for BTM vs grid-connected. Avoids grid tariffs, transmission losses, and regulatory tariff risk exposure.
premiums.source: Framework parameter: BTM structure with 15-25% NPV premium
premiums.baseForCalculation: Applied to power cost advantage NPV as premium layer

premiums.name: PUE Efficiency Premium
premiums.rationale: Framework explicitly states CHF 39M separate NPV line item for PUE 1.08 vs industry 1.50. Represents 28% lower energy overhead across facility life.
premiums.source: Framework parameter: CHF 39M separate NPV line
premiums.calculationNote: PUE saving = (1 - 1.08/1.50) × total energy cost. Framework has pre-calculated this at CHF 39M.

premiums.name: Exclusivity / Moat Premium
premiums.rationale: 5-year exclusive development rights prevent competition on this specific site. Combined with full permitting, creates a time-based moat. Premium decays as exclusivity period expires.
premiums.baseForCalculation: Applied as multiple premium to base EV/EBITDA

discounts.name: Pre-Revenue Discount
discounts.rationale: No current revenue stream. All financials are projections. Comparable operational assets trade at full multiples; pre-revenue assets require significant discount.

discounts.name: Founder / Execution Discount
discounts.rationale: No independent assessment of management team track record. Execution risk for CHF 375M+ CapEx program is substantial. Discount applied until team due diligence completed.

discounts.name: Single-Asset Concentration Discount
discounts.rationale: Single-site, single-jurisdiction concentration risk. Multi-site operators receive diversification premiums that single-asset projects lack.

netPremiumSummary.interpretation: Discounts slightly outweigh premiums on multiple basis. However, the power cost advantage NPV and PUE NPV are standalone value additions that operate independently of multiple adjustments.

sovereignAdjustedMultiple.methodology: Start from base EV/EBITDA and apply cumulative premium/discount adjustments
sovereignAdjustedMultiple.note: Net-adjusted multiple of 12.9× reflects heavy discounting from pre-revenue and execution risk. This is the 'current-state' multiple. The framework's 15-22× range represents 'at-revenue' multiples — the gap is the execution-stage discount.

infrastructureAdjustedMultiple.methodology: Infrastructure assets with contracted cash flows and regulated returns trade at premium to generic tech multiples. Adjust for infrastructure-grade characteristics.
infrastructureAdjustedMultiple.note: European data centre infrastructure EV/EBITDA range 12-18× for operational assets

adjustments.rationale: Contracted cost base mimics regulated utility

infrastructureAdjustedMultiple.interpretation: Infrastructure-adjusted multiple converges near 14× — premiums and discounts roughly cancel. Consistent with the sovereign-adjusted path.

riskMatrix.methodology: Probability (1-5) × Impact (1-5). Score ranges: 1-5 Low, 6-12 Medium, 13-19 High, 20-25 Critical.

risks.mitigation: Pre-revenue stage — entire valuation depends on achieving target utilisation
risks.scenarioImpact: Bear case + potential write-down if <50% utilisation

risks.mitigation: Swiss construction costs are well-documented; CHF 18M/MW bear case provides buffer
Contracted rate locks in floor — spread narrows but never inverts. Bear case models CHF 0.18/kWh market
risks.scenarioImpact: Bear case directly models this risk

risks.mitigation: Fully permitted, shovel-ready reduces but doesn't eliminate construction risk
risks.scenarioImpact: Delays push revenue start date, reducing NPV

risks.mitigation: PUE 1.08 is aggressive but achievable with alpine climate. PUE 1.15-1.20 still excellent.
risks.scenarioImpact: CHF 39M PUE NPV line item reduced proportionally

risks.mitigation: Swiss regulatory stability is a core thesis pillar. Low probability but material if occurs.
CHF 375-825M CapEx requirement is substantial. No disclosed funding status.
risks.scenarioImpact: Binary risk — unfunded project cannot proceed

risks.mitigation: 5-year window for Phase 1 + expansion decision. Tight but feasible.
risks.scenarioImpact: Moat premium evaporates; expansion optionality lost

riskSummary.interpretation: Two high-severity risks (customer acquisition and funding) are both pre-revenue stage risks. The core infrastructure thesis (power economics, permitting, jurisdiction) carries medium-to-low risk.

chartSpecifications.note: Specifications for Frontend Engineer to render visualisations

charts.title: Weighted Scoring — Category Performance
charts.colorScheme: Single color with opacity gradient by score
charts.annotations: Show percentage labels at each vertex

charts.title: Premium/Discount Attribution — Multiple Walk
charts.colorScheme: Green for increases, red for decreases, blue for start/end

charts.title: Power Cost Advantage NPV by Scenario
EV/MW Comparison Across Scenarios
charts.annotation: Industry range reference line at CHF 8-15M/MW

sensitivityAnalysis.methodology: One-factor-at-a-time sensitivity on base case EV, varying each key assumption ±20% while holding others constant.

factors.note: Multiple is the largest single driver — directly scales EV linearly
Margin drives EBITDA directly — tied with multiple for sensitivity
Capped at 100% on upside. Utilisation risk is asymmetric — more downside than upside.
Revenue/MW reflects pricing power and service mix

factors.factor: Market Power Rate (spread impact on premium NPV)
factors.rank: N/A — affects standalone NPV, not EV directly
factors.note: Market rate is the key driver of the power cost advantage NPV — the single largest premium.

sensitivityAnalysis.keyInsight: EV is equally sensitive to multiple, margin, and revenue/MW (all linear). Utilisation has asymmetric risk (capped upside). The power cost advantage NPV is most sensitive to market power rate movements — a factor outside management control.

confirmedData: Sovereign-grade counterparties (EKW, Alpiq, Axpo, BKW, Canton GR)
frameworkProjections: CHF 0.24/kWh market rate (base) — requires market validation
CHF 0.32/kWh market rate (high) — requires market validation
CHF 12-18M/MW CapEx range — Swiss benchmark, not quoted
55-65% EBITDA margin — projection at steady state
CHF 1.3M/MW revenue — projection at steady state
15-22× EV/EBITDA range — framework assertion, not comparable transaction data
unverifiedAssumptions: Management team capability and track record
Capital raising status and investor interest
Network connectivity quality at Graubünden site
15% effective tax rate (Swiss cantonal — not confirmed for this specific entity)
dataGaps: No comparable transaction data — awaiting DEE-17 Research Agent output
No customer LOIs or pipeline disclosure
No detailed construction budget or timeline
No funding structure or capital stack details
